items.q: Which item is removed first from a stack?
items.why: Students confuse stacks (LIFO - like a stack of plates) with queues (FIFO - like a checkout line). The 'stack of plates' analogy makes LIFO concrete.

items.q: Why must a recursive function have a base case?
items.correct: Without it, the function calls itself indefinitely, causing a stack overflow.
items.random_wrong: To make the function run faster.
items.why: Students treat the base case as optional ceremony. It's the TERMINATION CONDITION that stops recursion - each call adds a stack frame, and without termination the stack will overflow.

items.q: What does inheritance allow a subclass to do?
items.correct: Reuse and extend the attributes and methods of its superclass.
items.random_wrong: Copy code from any unrelated class.
items.why: Students think inheritance overrides everything. It actually REUSES the parent's members (which can be added to or overridden), creating an 'is-a' relationship.

items.q: Which is a high-level programming language?
items.why: Correct answer: Python. “Assembly” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Integrated Development Environment. “Internet Data Engine” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In Python, how many spaces are conventionally used for one level of indentation (PEP 8)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 spaces. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
